_comment_1: Replace every 0 with a real GID read from the ALLSTARTER template.
_comment_2: To find GIDs: `python osm2tiled.py inspect-template level_ALLSTARTER_usethistobuildnewlevels.tmx`, then open the template in the modkit's Tiled, click a tile and read its local ID + its tileset's firstgid (GID = firstgid + local ID).
_comment_3: Every entry is a LIST: several GIDs = visual variants picked at random (deterministic via --seed).

tiles._comment: 'map' layer (ground). ground = default fill for unclassified cells.
tiles._comment_edges: Optional road edge/corner system. Straight edges are placed on road cells whose NW/NE/SW/SE side borders non-road; corners where two adjacent sides are exposed. Isometric convention: NW edge = neighbor (x-1,y), NE = (y-1), SE = (x+1), SW = (y+1). With the game's t_alltiles tileset (firstgid 1), the assignments derived from the shipped levels are: edges NW/NE/SW/SE = gids 29/30/33/34, corners N/E/W/S = [103,101,109]/[104,102,112]/[107,105,121]/[108,106,124].
tiles._comment_crosswalks: Crosswalk pairs for junction approaches; with t_alltiles: x = [114,119], y = [115,118].

props._comment: 'props' layer. building_catalog: 'WxH' keys = prop footprint in tiles; the tool tiles OSM footprints with the largest fitting entries. Tiled anchor = bottom-left corner of the footprint. PREFER complete-building tiles or automapper prefab markers (automapper_prefabs house tiles): tiles that are corner parts of multi-tile assemblies look broken when scattered alone. Automapper markers have no game-side prop JSON - running the modkit's automapping in Tiled is then REQUIRED before export.

markers._comment: 'auto' mode = the tool places spawn/objective/exit on the road network (gids below required). 'preserve-template' mode = keeps the template's objectives layer as-is (requires --size template; falls back to auto otherwise) and makes the marker locations walkable. Default mode without gids: cells left at 0, to be placed manually in Tiled.

traps._comment: Optional: trap gid placed on every water cell ('traps' layer).

road_widths_m._comment: Overrides the default widths (meters), e.g. "residential": 9. At coarse scales (>= 12 m/tile), widen these so the road hierarchy stays readable.
